self.num_medusa_heads = kwargs.pop("num_medusa_heads", 0)
        self.medusa_tree = kwargs.pop("medusa_tree", None)

        if self.is_medusa and self.async_mode:
            raise IncompatibleConfigError("Medusa Decoding is not yet supported with async.")

        # Paged attention
        self.is_block_kv_layout = kwargs.pop("is_block_kv_layout", False)
        self.pa_num_blocks = kwargs.pop("pa_num_blocks", self.batch_size)
        self.pa_block_size = kwargs.pop("pa_block_size", self.seq_len)

        # Prefix caching
        self.is_prefix_caching = kwargs.pop("is_prefix_caching", False)

        # Windowed Context Encoding
        self.windowed_context_encoding_size = kwargs.pop("windowed_context_encoding_size", None)

        # Chunked prefill
        # When chunked prefill is enabled, max_context_length will be
        # used as chunk size. Batch size will be 1 for CTE because it
        # concatenates all requests along seq dim. cp_config.max_num_seqs
        # is used to denote how many sequences are there in one concatenated
        # request.
        self.chunked_prefill_config = kwargs.pop("chunked_prefill_config", None)
        if type(self.chunked_prefill_config) is dict:
            self.chunked_prefill_config = ChunkedPrefillConfig(
                **self.chunked_prefill_config
            )

        # KV Quantization
        self.kv_quant_config = kwargs.pop("kv_quant_config", None)
        if type(self.kv_quant_config) is dict:
            self.kv_quant_config = KVQuantizationConfig(
                **self.kv_quant_config
            )

        self.is_chunked_prefill = self.chunked_prefill_config is not None
        if self.is_chunked_prefill:
            assert (
                self.is_block_kv_layout
            ), "is_block_kv_layout has to be true for chunked prefill" \
                + " because it depends on block KV"
            if not self.chunked_prefill_config.tkg_model_enabled:
                assert (
                    self.batch_size == 1
                ), "batch_size has to be 1 for chunked prefill CTE model" \
                    + " because it concatenates all requests into one" \
                    + " long request"

        # Tensor replacement for debugging and accuracy verification
        tensor_replacement_config = kwargs.pop("tensor_replacement_config", None)
        if isinstance(tensor_replacement_config, dict):
            self.tensor_replacement_config = TensorReplacementConfig(**tensor_replacement_config)
        elif isinstance(tensor_replacement_config, TensorReplacementConfig):
            self.tensor_replacement_config = tensor_replacement_config
        else:
            if tensor_replacement_config is not None:  # Only warn if something invalid was passed
                warnings.warn(
                    f"tensor_replacement_config should be a TensorReplacementConfig object or dictionary, got {type(tensor_replacement_config)}. "
                    "Tensor replacemnet will be disabled."
                )
            self.tensor_replacement_config = None

        # Tensor capture for debugging and accuracy verification
        tensor_capture_config = kwargs.pop("tensor_capture_config", None)
        if isinstance(tensor_capture_config, dict):
            self.tensor_capture_config = TensorCaptureConfig(**tensor_capture_config)
        elif isinstance(tensor_capture_config, TensorCaptureConfig):
            self.tensor_capture_config = tensor_capture_config
        else:
            if tensor_capture_config is not None:  # Only warn if something invalid was passed
                warnings.warn(
                    f"tensor_capture_config should be a TensorCaptureConfig object or dictionary, got {type(tensor_capture_config)}. "
                    "Tensor capture will be disabled."
                )
            self.tensor_capture_config = None

        # Automatically set output_logits to True if tensor capture is enabled
        # This is required for tensor capture to work properly
        if self.tensor_capture_config and not self.output_logits:
            logging.info("Setting output_logits=True because tensor capture is enabled")
            self.output_logits = True
            if self.on_device_sampling_config is None:
                raise IncompatibleConfigError("Tensor capture requires on device sampling config to be set even if it's just defaults")

        # Lora
        self.lora_config = kwargs.pop("lora_config", None)
        if type(self.lora_config) is dict:
            self.lora_config = LoraServingConfig(**self.lora_config)

        # Distributed config
        self.tp_degree = kwargs.pop("tp_degree", 1)
        self.cp_degree = kwargs.pop("cp_degree", 1)
        self.mlp_cp_degree = kwargs.pop("mlp_cp_degree", 1)
        self.attention_dp_degree = kwargs.pop("attention_dp_degree", 1)
        self.pp_degree = kwargs.pop("pp_degree", 1)
        self.ep_degree = kwargs.pop("ep_degree", 1)
        self.save_sharded_checkpoint = kwargs.pop("save_sharded_checkpoint", False)
        self.skip_sharding = kwargs.pop("skip_sharding", False)
        self.enable_ve_data_parallel = kwargs.pop("enable_ve_data_parallel", False)

class ChunkedPrefillConfig:
    def __init__(self, **kwargs):
        # max_num_seqs is the actual batch size for chunked prefill
        self.max_num_seqs = kwargs.pop("max_num_seqs", 0)

        # Whether to enable a separate TKG model for decoding-only requests.
        # With chunked prefill, the CTE model is always enabled and it can
        # process both prefilling and decoding requests in one iteration,
        # while a separate TKG model is OPTIONAL. The seprate TKG model can
        # process decoding-only requests, but in a faster way for now.
        self.tkg_model_enabled = kwargs.pop("tkg_model_enabled", True)

        # query len of the tile to be used by the kernel
        self.kernel_q_tile_size = kwargs.pop("kernel_q_tile_size", 128)
        # kv cache len of the tile to be used by the kernel
        self.kernel_kv_tile_size = kwargs.pop("kernel_kv_tile_size", 1024)


class HybridShardingConfig:
    def __init__(self, **kwargs):
        self.moe_cte_tp_degree = kwargs.pop("moe_cte_tp_degree", 1)
        self.moe_cte_ep_degree = kwargs.pop("moe_cte_ep_degree", 1)
        self.moe_tkg_tp_degree = kwargs.pop("moe_tkg_tp_degree", 1)
        self.moe_tkg_ep_degree = kwargs.pop("moe_tkg_ep_degree", 1)


def get_platform_lnc():
    """
    Get the Logical NeuronCore Configuration (LNC) for the current platform.
    """
    warning_message = (
        "neuronx_distributed_inference.models.config.get_platform_lnc() is deprecated. "
        "Use neuronx_distributed.utils.model_utils.get_platform_lnc() instead."
    )
    warnings.warn(warning_message, category=DeprecationWarning)

    target = get_platform_target()
    if target == "trn2" or target == "trn3":
        return 2
    else:

def enable_context_encoding(self, **model_init_kwargs):
        new_config = copy.deepcopy(self.config)
        new_config.neuron_config.batch_size = self.neuron_config.ctx_batch_size
        if self.neuron_config.enable_fused_speculation:
            new_config.fused_spec_config.draft_config.neuron_config.batch_size = (
                self.neuron_config.ctx_batch_size
            )
            new_config.fused_spec_config.draft_config.neuron_config.is_prefill_stage = True
        new_config.neuron_config.n_active_tokens = self.neuron_config.max_context_length
        new_config.neuron_config.bucket_n_active_tokens = True
        new_config.neuron_config.is_prefill_stage = True

        new_config.neuron_config.buckets = autobucketing.generate_buckets_for_cte(new_config)

        self.context_encoding_model = self.model_wrapper(
            config=new_config,
            model_cls=self._model_cls,
            tag=CONTEXT_ENCODING_MODEL_TAG,
            compiler_args=self.get_compiler_args(),
            model_init_kwargs=model_init_kwargs,
        )
        self.models.append(self.context_encoding_model)

    def enable_token_generation(self, enable_wlt_optimization: bool = True, **model_init_kwargs):
        new_config = copy.deepcopy(self.config)
        new_config.neuron_config.batch_size = self.neuron_config.tkg_batch_size
        new_config.neuron_config.n_active_tokens = 1
        new_config.neuron_config.bucket_n_active_tokens = False
        new_config.neuron_config.sequence_parallel_enabled = False
        new_config.neuron_config.is_prefill_stage = False

        new_config.neuron_config.buckets = autobucketing.generate_buckets_for_tkg(new_config)

        # shouldn't be used in token gen models
        new_config.neuron_config.sequence_parallel_enabled = False

        self.token_generation_model = self.model_wrapper(
            config=new_config,
            model_cls=self._model_cls,
            tag=TOKEN_GENERATION_MODEL_TAG,
            compiler_args=self.get_compiler_args(),
            priority_model_idx=(
                0 if enable_wlt_optimization else None
            ),  # to turn on weight layout optimization
            model_init_kwargs=model_init_kwargs,
        )
        self.models.append(self.token_generation_model)

    def enable_speculation(self, enable_wlt_optimization: bool = True, **model_init_kwargs):
        new_config = copy.deepcopy(self.config)
        new_config.neuron_config.batch_size = self.neuron_config.spec_batch_size
        new_config.neuron_config.n_active_tokens = self.neuron_config.speculation_length
        new_config.neuron_config.bucket_n_active_tokens = False

        new_config.neuron_config.sequence_parallel_enabled = False
        new_config.neuron_config.is_prefill_stage = False

        new_config.neuron_config.buckets = autobucketing.generate_buckets_for_speculation(
            new_config
        )

        self.speculation_model = self.model_wrapper(
            config=new_config,
            model_cls=self._model_cls,
            tag=SPECULATION_MODEL_TAG,
            model_init_kwargs=model_init_kwargs,
            compiler_args=self.get_compiler_args(),
            priority_model_idx=(
                0 if enable_wlt_optimization else None
            ),  # to turn on weight layout optimization
        )

        self.models.append(self.speculation_model)

    def enable_medusa_speculation(self):
        new_config = copy.deepcopy(self.config)
        new_config.neuron_config.batch_size = self.neuron_config.spec_batch_size
        new_config.neuron_config.n_active_tokens = self.neuron_config.medusa_speculation_length
        self.medusa_speculation_model = self.model_wrapper(
            config=new_config, model_cls=self._model_cls, tag=MEDUSA_MODEL_TAG

file.content:
# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
import logging
from collections import deque
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING

from vllm.v1.core.sched.scheduler import Scheduler
from vllm.v1.core.sched.utils import check_stop
from vllm.v1.request import Request

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from vllm.v1.core.sched.output import SchedulerOutput
else:
    SchedulerOutput = None

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)


class NeuronScheduler(Scheduler):
    """Base class inheriting from the V1 scheduler to support continuous
    batching respecting Neuron constraints."""

    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs) -> None:
        # Initialize vLLM scheduler
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)

        # Requests are temporarily moved to this queue so that the base
        # scheduler does not see them. This lets us ensure that the set of
        # requests scheduled have at least one common warmup shape.
        self.holdback_queue: deque[Request] = deque()

    def _update_request_with_output(
        self,
        request: Request,
        new_token_ids: list[int],
    ) -> tuple[list[int], bool]:
        """
        Override to prevent assertion crash during speculative decoding.

        Issue: When using EAGLE/speculative decoding near max_model_len,
        the draft model adds extra tokens that can exceed max_model_len,
        causing 'Sampled token IDs exceed the max model length' assertion.

        Fix: Stop generation at (max_model_len - num_spec_tokens) to leave
        room for speculative tokens.
        """
        stopped = False
        spec_len = getattr(self, "num_spec_tokens", 0)
        effective_max_len = max(self.max_model_len - spec_len, 0)

        for num_new, output_token_id in enumerate(new_token_ids, 1):
            request.append_output_token_ids(output_token_id)

            stopped = check_stop(request, effective_max_len)
            if stopped:
                del new_token_ids[num_new:]
                break
        return new_token_ids, stopped


class ContinuousBatchingNeuronScheduler(NeuronScheduler):
    """Support of continuous batching"""

    # inherited from V1 base scheduler but mypy needs to know the type
    running: list[Request]

    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs) -> None:
        # Initialize NeuronScheduler
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)

    def schedule(self) -> "SchedulerOutput":
        """This override adds constraints and then delegates most of the work
        to the base scheduler

        To avoid additional specialization, some requests are held back from the
        base scheduler but are restored after.
        """

        # First purge the full waiting queue into our holdback queue, preserving
        # priority
        while self.waiting:
            self.holdback_queue.append(self.waiting.popleft())

        # Check if new requests can be scheduled.
        while self.holdback_queue:
            if self.can_schedule(self.holdback_queue[0]):
                # Add request to the waiting queue
                self.waiting.append(self.holdback_queue.popleft())
            else:
                # Otherwise, we simply stop here so that the scheduler
                # can work with the batch we have
                break

        # Schedule Prefill and Decode separately
        if len(self.waiting) > 0:
            # For prefill, hide current decodes from the scheduler
            running_holdback = self.running
            self.running = []
            logger.debug(
                "Scheduling a prefill step of %s requests, holding back %s requests",
                len(self.waiting),
                len(self.holdback_queue),
            )
        else:
            running_holdback = []
            logger.debug("Scheduling a decode step of %s requests", len(self.running))

        # delegate to super of NeuronScheduler: base V1 Scheduler
        outputs = super(NeuronScheduler, self).schedule()

        # restore holdbacks after running the base scheduler
        self.running = self.running + running_holdback
        while self.holdback_queue:
            self.waiting.append(self.holdback_queue.popleft())

        return outputs

    def can_schedule(self, request) -> bool:
        max_prompt_batch_size = 1
        _max_context_len = self.max_model_len

        # running and waiting queues are both empty -> start new batch
        start_new_batch = len(self.running) + len(self.waiting) == 0
        # check that there is space in the current decode batch
        cond1 = len(self.running) + len(self.waiting) < self.max_num_running_reqs
        # check that there is space in the prefill batch
        cond2 = len(self.waiting) < max_prompt_batch_size
        # TODO: add context length checks
        return start_new_batch or (cond1 and cond2)

                    # We use `request.num_tokens` instead of

                    num_new_tokens = request.num_tokens - num_computed_tokens
                    threshold = self.scheduler_config.long_prefill_token_threshold
                    if 0 < threshold < num_new_tokens:

            input_capture_hook=input_capture_hook,
            tensor_capture_hook=tensor_capture_hook,
            slot_mapping=slot_mapping,
            block_table=block_table,
            full_context_lens=full_context_lens,
            computed_context_lens=computed_context_lens)

elif self.neuron_config.is_block_kv_layout:
            outputs, is_run_on_neuron = self._get_model_outputs(
                input_ids,
                attention_mask,
                position_ids,
                seq_ids,
                sampling_params,
                prev_hidden,
                adapter_ids,
                medusa_args,
                llava_args,
                slot_mapping,
                block_table,
                full_context_lens,
                computed_context_lens,
            )
        else:
            outputs, is_run_on_neuron = self._get_model_outputs(
                input_ids,
                attention_mask,
                position_ids,
                seq_ids,
                sampling_params,
                prev_hidden,
                adapter_ids,
                medusa_args,
                llava_args,
                tf_args=tf_args
            )

        generation_model = self.get_generation_model()
        if not generation_model.is_neuron():
            self._copy_past_key_values(outputs)

        # Get processed and constructed outputs
        constructed_outputs = self._get_constructed_outputs(outputs, is_run_on_neuron)

        # Apply tensor_capture_hook if provided and tensors are captured
        if tensor_capture_hook and constructed_outputs.captured_tensors:
            # Apply the hook if captured tensors are found
            tensor_capture_hook(self, constructed_outputs.captured_tensors)

        if logging.root.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG):
            logging.debug("---output---")
            logging.debug(
                f"{'tokens' if self.on_device_sampling else 'logits'} = %s, ",
                constructed_outputs.logits,
            )

        return constructed_outputs

    def validate_sampling_params(self, params: torch.Tensor) -> None:
        if self.on_device_sampling:
            # Call validate_sampling_params from the Sampler.
            validate_sampling_params(params, self.neuron_config.on_device_sampling_config)

    def _setup_func_config(self, output_attentions, output_hidden_states, return_dict):
        output_attentions = (
            output_attentions
            if output_attentions is not None
            else self.text_config.output_attentions
        )
        output_hidden_states = (
            output_hidden_states
            if output_hidden_states is not None
            else self.text_config.output_hidden_states
        )
        return_dict = (
            return_dict
            if return_dict is not None
            else getattr(self.config, "use_return_dict", None)
        )
        return output_attentions, output_hidden_states, return_dict

    def _infer_attention_mask(
        self,
        position_ids,
        full_context_lens: Optional[torch.LongTensor] = None,
        computed_context_lens: Optional[torch.LongTensor] = None,
    ):
        assert (
            position_ids is not None
        ), "need to call forward with position_ids if attention_mask is not provided"

        batch_size, seq_len = position_ids.shape
        if position_ids.shape[-1] != 1:
            seq_len = position_ids.shape[-1]
            position_ids_to_compare = position_ids
        else:
            seq_len = torch.max(position_ids)
            position_ids_to_compare = position_ids.expand(batch_size, seq_len) - 1
        mask = torch.arange(seq_len).view(1, -1).expand(batch_size, seq_len)
        attention_mask = (position_ids_to_compare >= mask).to(dtype=position_ids.dtype)
        return attention_mask

    def _log_input(
        self, input_ids, attention_mask, position_ids, seq_ids, adapter_ids=None, **kwargs
    ):
        logging.debug("---input---")
        logging.debug("input_ids shape = %s type=%s", input_ids.shape, input_ids.type())

def _get_model_outputs(
        self,
        input_ids,
        attention_mask,
        position_ids,
        seq_ids,
        sampling_params,
        prev_hidden,
        adapter_ids,
        medusa_args,
        llava_args,
        slot_mapping=None,
        block_table=None,
        full_context_lens=None,
        computed_context_lens=None,
        tf_args=None
    ):
        if self.neuron_config.is_prefix_caching:
            num_queries = full_context_lens - computed_context_lens
            # Expect active and ordered block table for each seq after this step
            batch_size, _ = num_queries.shape

            is_context_encoding = input_ids.shape[-1] > 1 and not position_ids.min().item()
            generation_model = self.fused_spec_model if self.neuron_config.enable_fused_speculation else self.token_generation_model

            self.base_model = (
                self.context_encoding_model if is_context_encoding else generation_model
            )
            if self.neuron_config.enable_eagle_speculation:
                outputs = self.base_model(
                    input_ids,
                    attention_mask,
                    position_ids,
                    seq_ids,
                    sampling_params,
                    torch.empty(0),
                    adapter_ids,
                    slot_mapping,
                    block_table,
                    num_queries,
                    computed_context_lens,
                    torch.empty(0),
                    torch.empty(0),
                    torch.empty(0),
                    torch.empty(0),
                    torch.empty(0),
                    *llava_args,
                )
            elif self.neuron_config.enable_fused_speculation:
                outputs = self.base_model(
                    input_ids,
                    attention_mask,
                    position_ids,
                    seq_ids,
                    sampling_params,
                    torch.empty(0),
                    adapter_ids,
                    slot_mapping,
                    block_table,
                    num_queries,
                    computed_context_lens,
                    *llava_args,
                )
            else:
                outputs = self.base_model(
                    input_ids,
                    attention_mask,
                    position_ids,
                    seq_ids,
                    sampling_params,
                    torch.empty(0),
                    adapter_ids,
                    torch.empty(0),
                    torch.empty(0),
                    torch.empty(0),
                    torch.empty(0),
                    slot_mapping,
                    block_table,
                    num_queries,
                    computed_context_lens,
                    *llava_args,
                )
            is_run_on_neuron = self.base_model.is_neuron()
        elif self._is_prefill(position_ids):
            if self.neuron_config.is_medusa:
                medusa_args = self._prepare_inputs()
                outputs = self.context_encoding_model(
                    input_ids,
                    attention_mask,
                    position_ids,
                    seq_ids,
                    sampling_params,
                    prev_hidden,
                    adapter_ids,
                    *medusa_args,
                )
            elif self.neuron_config.tensor_replacement_config:
                empties = [torch.empty(0) for i in range(18)]
                outputs = self.context_encoding_model(
                    input_ids,
                    attention_mask,
                    position_ids,
                    seq_ids,
                    sampling_params,
                    prev_hidden,
                    adapter_ids,
                    *empties,
                    *llava_args,
                    *tf_args
                )
            else:
                outputs = self.context_encoding_model(
                    input_ids,
                    attention_mask,
                    position_ids,
                    seq_ids,
                    sampling_params,
                    prev_hidden,
                    adapter_ids,
                    *llava_args,

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
"""A Neuron worker class."""

from typing import Set

import torch.nn as nn
from vllm.config import VllmConfig
from vllm.distributed import (
    ensure_model_parallel_initialized,
    init_distributed_environment,
)
from vllm.distributed.kv_transfer import (
    ensure_kv_transfer_initialized,
    has_kv_transfer_group,
)
from vllm.logger import init_logger
from vllm.lora.request import LoRARequest
from vllm.utils.torch_utils import set_random_seed
from vllm.tasks import GenerationTask, SupportedTask
from vllm.v1.core.sched.output import SchedulerOutput, GrammarOutput
from vllm.v1.kv_cache_interface import KVCacheConfig, KVCacheSpec
from vllm.v1.outputs import DraftTokenIds, ModelRunnerOutput
from vllm.v1.worker.worker_base import WorkerBase

logger = init_logger(__name__)


class NeuronWorker(WorkerBase):
    """A worker class that executes the model on a group of neuron cores."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        vllm_config: VllmConfig,
        local_rank: int,
        rank: int,
        distributed_init_method: str,
        is_driver_worker: bool = False,
    ) -> None:
        # In vLLM 0.16.0+, verify_with_parallel_config runs before check_and_update_config from platform.py so applying patches here ensures they're active
        from vllm_neuron.platform import NeuronPlatform

        NeuronPlatform._apply_config_overrides()

        super().__init__(
            vllm_config=vllm_config,
            local_rank=local_rank,
            rank=rank,
            distributed_init_method=distributed_init_method,
            is_driver_worker=is_driver_worker,
        )

        if self.model_config.trust_remote_code:
            # note: lazy import to avoid importing torch before initializing
            from transformers.dynamic_module_utils import init_hf_modules

            init_hf_modules()
        self.device = self.device_config.device
        self.model_runner = self.get_neuronx_distributed_model_runner(
            vllm_config, self.device
        )
        self.vllm_config = vllm_config

    def init_device(self) -> None:
        self.init_distributed_environment(self.vllm_config)

        # Set random seed.
        set_random_seed(self.model_config.seed)

    def _query_runtime_memory(self) -> int:
        """Query Neuron runtime for available memory."""
        import torch

        try:
            rt = torch.classes.neuron.Runtime()
            bytes_used, bytes_free = rt.get_vnc_memory_stats()
            if bytes_used == -1 and bytes_free == -1:
                raise RuntimeError("Failed to initialize neuron runtime")
            logger.debug(
                "Device memory stats: %s bytes used, %s bytes free (%.2f GB free)",
                bytes_used,
                bytes_free,
                bytes_free / (1024**3) if bytes_free > 0 else 0,
            )
            return bytes_free
        except Exception as e:
            logger.debug("Failed to get memory stats: %s", e)
            return 20 * 1024**3  # 20GB fallback

    def _calculate_contiguous_kv_memory(self) -> int:
        """
        Calculate memory size for contiguous KV cache blocks.

        Returns memory corresponding to max_num_seqs + 1 blocks,
        where +1 accounts for vLLM's null_block reservation.
        """
        from vllm.utils.torch_utils import get_dtype_size
        from vllm_neuron.worker.utils import get_num_layers_from_hf_config

        max_num_seqs = self.vllm_config.scheduler_config.max_num_seqs
        num_blocks = max_num_seqs + 1  # +1 for null_block

content.content:
100	        num_blocks = max_num_seqs + 1  # +1 for null_block
101	
102	        num_layers = get_num_layers_from_hf_config(self.model_config.hf_config)
103	        block_size = self.cache_config.block_size
104	        head_size = self.model_config.get_head_size()
105	        dtype_size = get_dtype_size(self.model_config.dtype)
106	        num_kv_heads = self.parallel_config.tensor_parallel_size
107	
108	        page_size_per_layer = 2 * block_size * num_kv_heads * head_size * dtype_size
109	        available_memory = num_blocks * num_layers * page_size_per_layer
110	
111	        logger.debug(
112	            "Contiguous KV cache: %d bytes (%.2f GB) for %d blocks",
113	            available_memory,
114	            available_memory / (1024**3),
115	            num_blocks,
116	        )
117	        return available_memory
118	
119	    def determine_available_memory(self):
120	        """
121	        For block KV layout, we query Neuron runtime for available memory.
122	        When running models using a contiguous KV cache layout on Neuron, we need
123	        exactly max_num_seqs number of blocks, where each block size is equal to
124	        max_model_len. Thus we manually calculate the memory size that this many
125	        blocks would correspond to.
126	
127	        """
128	        if self.model_runner.is_block_kv_layout:
129	            return self._query_runtime_memory()
130	
131	        return self._calculate_contiguous_kv_memory()
132	
133	    def execute_model(
134	        self, scheduler_output: "SchedulerOutput"
135	    ) -> ModelRunnerOutput | None:
136	        output = self.model_runner.execute_model(scheduler_output)
137	
138	        return output if self.is_driver_worker or has_kv_transfer_group() else None
139	
140	    def profile(self, is_start: bool = True):
141	        raise NotImplementedError
142	
143	    def get_neuronx_distributed_model_runner(self, vllm_config, device):
144	        from vllm_neuron.worker.neuronx_distributed_model_runner import (
145	            NeuronxDistributedModelRunner,
146	        )
147	
148	        return NeuronxDistributedModelRunner(vllm_config=vllm_config, device=device)
149	
150	    def initialize_cache(self, num_gpu_blocks: int, num_cpu_blocks: int) -> None:
151	        self.cache_config.num_gpu_blocks = num_gpu_blocks
152	        self.cache_config.num_cpu_blocks = num_cpu_blocks
153	
154	    def load_model(self):
155	        self.model_runner.load_model()
156	
157	    def compile_or_warm_up_model(self) -> None:
158	        # Skip for NeuronX Distributed Inference
159	        return None
160	
161	    def get_model(self) -> nn.Module:
162	        raise NotImplementedError
163	
164	    def get_kv_cache_spec(self) -> dict[str, KVCacheSpec]:
165	        return self.model_runner.get_kv_cache_spec()
166	
167	    def initialize_from_config(self, kv_cache_config: KVCacheConfig) -> None:
168	        """Allocate GPU KV cache with the specified kv_cache_config."""
169	        self.model_runner.initialize_kv_cache(kv_cache_config)
170	
171	    def check_health(self) -> None:
172	        # worker will always be healthy as long as it's running.
173	        return
174	
175	    def init_distributed_environment(self, vllm_config: VllmConfig):
176	        """
177	        vLLM still needs the environment initialized when TP/PP > 1
178	        """
179	
180	        from vllm.config import ParallelConfig
181	
182	        from vllm_neuron.platform import NeuronPlatform
183	
184	        NeuronPlatform._apply_world_size_override(ParallelConfig)
185	
186	        init_distributed_environment(
187	            world_size=1,
188	            rank=self.rank,
189	            local_rank=self.local_rank,
190	            distributed_init_method=self.distributed_init_method,
191	            backend="gloo",
192	        )
193	
194	        ensure_model_parallel_initialized(
195	            1,
196	            1,
197	        )
198	
199	        ensure_kv_transfer_initialized(vllm_config)
200	
201	    def shutdown(self) -> None:
202	        self.model_runner.ensure_kv_transfer_shutdown()
203	
204	    # vLLM uses add_adapter() to add LoRA, rather than add_lora()
205	    def add_adapter(self, lora_request: LoRARequest) -> bool:
206	        # Todo: support dynamic add/remove lora
207	        return
208	
209	    def add_lora(self, lora_request: LoRARequest) -> bool:
210	        return self.model_runner.add_lora(lora_request)
211	
212	    def remove_lora(self, lora_id: int) -> bool:
213	        return self.model_runner.remove_lora(lora_id)
214	
215	    def pin_lora(self, lora_id: int) -> bool:
216	        return self.model_runner.pin_lora(lora_id)
217	
218	    def list_loras(self) -> Set[int]:
219	        return self.model_runner.list_loras()

1	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2	"""
3	A model loader implementation for NeuronX Distributed Inference (NxDI).
4	
5	This class serves as the primary interface for loading and managing
6	machine learning models optimized for AWS Neuron hardware. It provides
7	functionality for:
8	    - Loading pre-trained models and their configurations
9	    - Managing model compilation
10	    - Handling distributed inference across multiple Neuron cores
11	    - Supporting various model architectures and configurations
12	    - Managing key-value caches for optimized inference
13	    - Implementing sampling strategies for model outputs
14	
15	The loader supports various model architectures and can be extended to handle
16	different model types and configurations. It integrates with the broader
17	vLLM framework while providing specific optimizations for AWS Neuron hardware.
18	"""
19	
20	import collections
21	import copy
22	import hashlib
23	import logging

61	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
62	logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)
63	
64	
65	class NeuronModelBase(nn.Module):
66	    """
67	    Base class for all Neuron models.
68	    It is used to load the model, run the model, and sample the model.
69	    It is also used to get the KV caches.
70	    """
71	
72	    def __init__(self, config: PretrainedConfig) -> None:
73	        super().__init__()
74	        self.logits_processor = LogitsProcessor(
75	            config.get_text_config().vocab_size, logits_as_input=True
76	        )
77	        self.on_device_sampling_disabled = bool(
78	            int(os.getenv("NEURON_ON_DEVICE_SAMPLING_DISABLED", "0"))
79	        )
80	        if self.on_device_sampling_disabled:
81	            self.sampler = Sampler()
82	
83	        # Lazy initialized
84	        self.model: nn.Module
85	        self.kv_caches: list[Any] | None = None
86	        self.neuron_config: NeuronConfig
87	        self.is_reorder_needed: bool
88	        self.architecture: str
89	        self.num_key_value_heads: int
90	        self.head_size: int
91	        self.dtype: torch.dtype
92	
93	    def forward(self, input_ids, positions, input_block_ids, sampling_params, **kwargs):
94	        raise NotImplementedError
95	
96	    def sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> SamplerOutput | None:
97	        raise NotImplementedError
98	
99	    def load_weights(self, model_name_or_path: str, architecture: str, **kwargs):
100	        raise NotImplementedError
101	
102	    def get_kv_caches(self):
103	        if self.kv_caches is None:
104	            kv_caches = []
105	            tp_tensors_map = collections.defaultdict(list)
106	            state = self.model.context_encoding_model.model.nxd_model.state
107	
108	            for tp_idx, per_tp_state in enumerate(state):
109	                for key, val in per_tp_state.items():
110	                    tp_tensors_map[tp_idx].append(val)
111	
112	            for i in range(len(tp_tensors_map[0])):
113	                for tp, tensors in tp_tensors_map.items():
114	                    kv_caches.append(tensors[i])
115	            self.kv_caches = kv_caches
116	
117	        return self.kv_caches
118	
119	    @contextmanager
120	    def _reordered(self, input_block_ids: torch.Tensor, **inputs):
121	        """
122	        Context manager that yields reordered input_block_ids, inputs, and a restore function.
123	        Automatically restores output to original order if needed.
124	
125	        [NOTE] This is MANDATORY for contiguous kv cache as it will impact the output accuracy.
126	
127	        TODO: This sequence id reordering is better to live in NxD-Inference.
128	        """
129	        logger.debug("is_reorder_needed: %s", self.is_reorder_needed)
130	        if self.is_reorder_needed:
131	            sort_start = time.perf_counter()
132	            sorted_ids, sorted_indices = torch.sort(input_block_ids)
133	            sort_elapsed = (time.perf_counter() - sort_start) * 1000
134	            logger.debug("[PERF]       torch.sort: %.2fms", sort_elapsed)
135	
136	            sort_inputs_start = time.perf_counter()
137	            reordered_inputs = self._sort_inputs(inputs, sorted_indices)
138	            sort_inputs_elapsed = (time.perf_counter() - sort_inputs_start) * 1000
139	            logger.debug("[PERF]       _sort_inputs: %.2fms", sort_inputs_elapsed)
140	
141	            def restore(output: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
142	                if sorted_ids.shape[0] != 1:
143	                    return torch.index_select(output, 0, torch.argsort(sorted_indices))
144	                return output
145	
146	            yield sorted_ids, reordered_inputs, restore
147	        else:
148	            yield input_block_ids, inputs, lambda x: x
149	
150	    @staticmethod
151	    def _sort_inputs(
152	        inputs: dict[str, Any], sorted_indices: torch.Tensor
153	    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
154	        """Apply sorting to a dict of tensor/list inputs along batch dimension."""
155	        sorted_inputs = {}
156	        for k, v in inputs.items():
157	            if isinstance(v, torch.Tensor):
158	                if v.shape[0] > 0:  # avoid empty tensors
159	                    if (
160	                        v.shape[0] != sorted_indices.shape[0]
161	                    ):  # mm inputs are only sorted during prefill
162	                        logger.debug(
163	                            "Skipping reorder for key %s which has batch size %s but sorted_indices has len %s",
164	                            k,
165	                            v.shape[0],
166	                            sorted_indices.shape[0],
167	                        )
168	                        sorted_inputs[k] = v
169	                        continue
170	                    sorted_inputs[k] = torch.index_select(v, 0, sorted_indices)
171	                else:
172	                    sorted_inputs[k] = v
173	            elif isinstance(v, list):
174	                sorted_inputs[k] = [v[i.item()] for i in sorted_indices]
175	            else:
176	                sorted_inputs[k] = v
177	        return sorted_inputs
178	
179	    def _load_weights_common(
180	        self, model_name_or_path: str, neuronx_model_cls, **kwargs
181	    ):
182	        # ===== Extract draft-specific config BEFORE creating any NeuronConfig =====
183	        neuron_config_dict = kwargs["neuron_config"]
184	        draft_overrides = neuron_config_dict.pop("draft_overrides", None)
185	        # ===== END EXTRACTION =====
186	
187	        neuron_config = neuronx_model_cls.get_neuron_config_cls()(**neuron_config_dict)
188	
189	        config = kwargs.get("config") or neuronx_model_cls.get_config_cls()(
190	            neuron_config, load_config=load_pretrained_config(model_name_or_path)
191	        )
192	
193	        # If fused speculation is enabled, attach the draft model config.
194	        if getattr(neuron_config, "enable_fused_speculation", False):
195	            assert kwargs.get("speculative_config") is not None, (
196	                "Must pass speculative_config to load weights if using Neuron Speculation."
197	            )
198	            self._init_fused_spec_config(
199	                config,
200	                neuron_config_dict,  # Already cleaned
201	                neuronx_model_cls,
202	                kwargs["speculative_config"],
203	                draft_overrides,
204	            )
205	        hashed_config = hashlib.md5(config.to_json_string().encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
206	        compiled_model_path = self._get_compiled_model_path(
207	            model_name_or_path, hashed_config
208	        )
209	
210	        try:
211	            self._load_compiled_model(compiled_model_path, neuronx_model_cls, kwargs)
212	            return True, compiled_model_path, config
213	        except (FileNotFoundError, ValueError) as e:
214	            logger.warning("Exception: %s", e)
215	            logger.warning(
216	                "Unable to find precompiled artifacts from %s. Recompiling...",
217	                compiled_model_path,
218	            )
219	            return False, compiled_model_path, config

221	    def _get_compiled_model_path(self, model_name_or_path: str, hashed_config: str):
222	        if os.getenv("NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS"):
223	            return os.getenv("NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS")
224	        elif os.path.exists(model_name_or_path):
225	            path = (
226	                Path(model_name_or_path) / "neuron-compiled-artifacts" / hashed_config
227	            )
228	            path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
229	            shutil.rmtree(path, ignore_errors=True)
230	            return path
231	        else:
232	            path = (
233	                Path("local-models")
234	                / model_name_or_path
235	                / "neuron-compiled-artifacts"
236	                / hashed_config
237	            )
238	            path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
239	            shutil.rmtree(path, ignore_errors=True)
240	            return path
241	
242	    def _load_compiled_model(self, compiled_model_path: str, neuronx_model_cls, kwargs):
243	        self.model = neuronx_model_cls(compiled_model_path)
244	
245	        self.model.load(compiled_model_path)
246	        logger.info(
247	            "Successfully loaded pre-compiled model artifacts from %s",
248	            compiled_model_path,
249	        )
250	        # When loading a pre-compiled model don't do any more overrides.
251	        override_neuron_config = kwargs.get("override_neuron_config")
252	        if override_neuron_config:
253	            logger.warning(
254	                "Using pre-compiled artifacts, override_neuron_config will be ignored"
255	            )
256	
257	    def _save_pretrained_model(self, model_name: str):
258	        hf_model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(model_name)
259	        saved_path = os.path.join("local-models", model_name)
260	        hf_model.save_pretrained(saved_path)
261	        return saved_path
262	
263	    def _compile_and_load_model(
264	        self, model_path: str, neuronx_model_cls, config, compiled_path: str
265	    ):
266	        self.model = neuronx_model_cls(model_path, config)
267	        # Quantize model.
268	        if config.neuron_config.quantized:
269	            neuronx_model_cls.save_quantized_state_dict(model_path, config)
270	        self.model.compile(compiled_path)
271	        self.model.load(compiled_path)

273	    def _init_fused_spec_config(
274	        self,
275	        config,
276	        neuron_config_dict,
277	        neuronx_model_cls,
278	        speculative_config,
279	        draft_overrides=None,
280	    ):
281	        """
282	        Initialize and attach a `fused_spec_config` to the target model's
283	        NeuronXDistributed config when fused speculation is enabled.
284	
285	        Supports both homogeneous (same architecture) and heterogeneous
286	        (different architectures) draft models via automatic architecture detection.
287	
288	        Behavior:
289	        • Deep copy the target's instantiated `neuron_config` object to
290	        build draft config (preserves all fields without manual filtering).
291	        • Force-disable `enable_fused_speculation` in the draft to prevent
292	        recursion (only the target should run fused speculation).
293	        • Zero out `speculation_length` for the draft unless EAGLE is used,
294	        since the target controls speculation length.
295	        • Apply `draft_model_modules_to_not_convert` if specified.
296	        • For EAGLE drafts, mark `is_eagle_draft=True`.
297	        • Apply user-provided `draft_overrides` as attributes to handle
298	        architecture differences (e.g., different hidden sizes, kernels).
299	        • Auto-detect draft model class from checkpoint's config.json.
300	        • Load the draft HF config and wrap everything in a
301	        `FusedSpecNeuronConfig`, which is then attached to the target's config.
302	
303	        Draft Model Class Resolution:
304	            1. Auto-detect from draft checkpoint's config.json `architectures` field
305	            2. Fallback to target model class if detection fails (homogeneous speculation)
306	
307	        Args:
308	            config: The target model's NxDI config (will be modified in place
309	                    to attach `fused_spec_config`).
310	            neuron_config_dict: Original neuron configuration dictionary from
311	                    vLLM's `override_neuron_config` (used to extract draft settings).
312	            neuronx_model_cls: The NxDI model class for the target model, providing
313	                    config loaders. Used as draft class if auto-detection fails.
314	            speculative_config: vLLM's `SpeculativeConfig` describing the draft
315	                    model path and speculation parameters.
316	            draft_overrides: Optional dict of draft-specific config overrides applied
317	                    as attributes. Use to specify architecture differences (e.g.,
318	                    `{"padded_intermediate_size": 14336, "mlp_kernel_enabled": True}`).
319	                    Only specify fields that differ from target.
320	
321	        Raises:
322	            ValueError: If draft config cannot be loaded from checkpoint.
323	
324	        Notes:
325	            Only the *target* model should have
326	            `neuron_config.enable_fused_speculation=True`. The draft must not,
327	            otherwise NxDI would attempt to compile nested fused speculation.
328	
329	            Configuration inheritance: Draft inherits ALL target fields via deep copy,
330	            then selectively overrides specific fields via `draft_overrides`.
331	        """
332	        # ===== KEY: Deep copy the CREATED target neuron_config object =====
333	        # This preserves all target-specific fields without needing to filter
334	        draft_neuron_config = copy.deepcopy(config.neuron_config)
335	
336	        # Modify specific draft attributes (reference pattern)
337	        if not getattr(config.neuron_config, "enable_eagle_speculation", False):
338	            draft_neuron_config.speculation_length = 0
339	
340	        draft_neuron_config.enable_fused_speculation = False
341	
342	        if getattr(config.neuron_config, "draft_model_modules_to_not_convert", None):
343	            draft_neuron_config.modules_to_not_convert = (
344	                config.neuron_config.draft_model_modules_to_not_convert
345	            )
346	
347	        if getattr(config.neuron_config, "enable_eagle_speculation", False):
348	            draft_neuron_config.is_eagle_draft = True
349	
350	        # Apply draft overrides as attributes (not dict update)
351	        if draft_overrides:
352	            for key, value in draft_overrides.items():
353	                setattr(draft_neuron_config, key, value)
354	
355	        # ===== Load draft config for architecture inspection =====
356	        logger.info("Inspecting draft model config from: %s", speculative_config.model)
357	        try:
358	            from transformers import AutoConfig
359	
360	            draft_hf_config_for_inspection = AutoConfig.from_pretrained(
361	                speculative_config.model, trust_remote_code=False
362	            )
363	            logger.debug(
364	                "Draft config architectures: %s",
365	                getattr(draft_hf_config_for_inspection, "architectures", None),
366	            )
367	        except Exception as e:
368	            logger.error("Failed to load draft config for inspection: %s", e)
369	            raise ValueError(
370	                f"Cannot load draft model config from '{speculative_config.model}'. "
371	                f"Please ensure the path is correct and config.json exists. "
372	                f"Error: {e}"
373	            ) from e
374	
375	        # ===== Auto-detect draft model class =====
376	        draft_neuron_model_cls = None
377	        auto_detected_cls = None
378	
379	        draft_arch = (
380	            draft_hf_config_for_inspection.architectures[0]
381	            if hasattr(draft_hf_config_for_inspection, "architectures")
382	            and draft_hf_config_for_inspection.architectures
383	            else None
384	        )
385	
386	        # Check for user-provided draft_neuron_model_class override
387	        draft_class_override = None
388	        if draft_overrides and "draft_neuron_model_class" in draft_overrides:
389	            draft_class_override = draft_overrides.pop("draft_neuron_model_class")
390	            logger.info(
391	                "User provided draft_neuron_model_class override: '%s'",
392	                draft_class_override,
393	            )
394	
395	        if draft_arch:
396	            logger.info(
397	                "Auto-detected draft architecture from config.json: %s", draft_arch
398	            )
399	            try:
400	                auto_detected_cls = _get_neuron_model_cls(draft_arch)
401	                logger.info(
402	                    "Auto-detected Neuron model class: %s",
403	                    auto_detected_cls.__name__,
404	                )
405	            except ValueError as e:
406	                logger.warning(
407	                    "Draft architecture '%s' is not supported on Neuron. Error: %s",
408	                    draft_arch,
409	                    e,
410	                )
411	        else:
412	            logger.warning(
413	                "No 'architectures' field found in draft config at %s.",
414	                speculative_config.model,
415	            )
416	
417	        # Resolve final draft model class: override > auto-detected > fallback
418	        if draft_class_override:
419	            try:
420	                draft_neuron_model_cls = _get_neuron_model_cls(draft_class_override)
421	                if auto_detected_cls:
422	                    logger.info(
423	                        "Using OVERRIDE class '%s' (auto-detected was '%s')",
424	                        draft_neuron_model_cls.__name__,
425	                        auto_detected_cls.__name__,
426	                    )
427	                else:
428	                    logger.info(
429	                        "Using OVERRIDE class '%s'",
430	                        draft_neuron_model_cls.__name__,
431	                    )
432	            except ValueError as e:
433	                logger.warning(
434	                    "Override class '%s' not found: %s. Falling back.",
435	                    draft_class_override,
436	                    e,
437	                )
438	                draft_neuron_model_cls = auto_detected_cls
439	        else:
440	            draft_neuron_model_cls = auto_detected_cls
441	
442	        # Fallback to target class if still None
443	        if draft_neuron_model_cls is None:
444	            draft_neuron_model_cls = neuronx_model_cls
445	            logger.info(
446	                "Falling back to target model class for draft: %s",
447	                draft_neuron_model_cls.__name__,
448	            )
449	
450	        # ===== Create draft config using load_pretrained_config (CALLABLE) =====
451	        logger.info(
452	            "Creating draft config with class: %s", draft_neuron_model_cls.__name__
453	        )
454	
455	        draft_config = draft_neuron_model_cls.get_config_cls()(
456	            draft_neuron_config,
457	            load_config=load_pretrained_config(speculative_config.model),
458	        )
459	
460	        # ===== Create and attach fused spec config =====
461	        fused_spec_config = FusedSpecNeuronConfig(
462	            neuronx_model_cls._model_cls,
463	            draft_config=draft_config,
464	            draft_model_path=speculative_config.model,
465	            draft_model_cls=draft_neuron_model_cls,
466	        )
467	
468	        config.fused_spec_config = fused_spec_config
469	        logger.info("Fused speculation config initialized successfully")
470	
471	
472	class NeuronCausalLM(NeuronModelBase):
473	    def _remask_fused_spec_output(self, fused, inputs):
474	        """
475	        Handle NxDI fused speculation output.
476	
477	        NxDI fused spec returns:
478	        fused[0] = accepted_tokens_with_padding : [B, T], 0-padded
479	        fused[-1] = next_pos_ids
480	
481	        We convert the 0-padding to -1 past the number of tokens actually
482	        generated in this step, so the runner can strip pads.
483	        """
484	        start_time = time.perf_counter()
485	
486	        accepted_tokens_with_padding = fused[0]
487	        next_pos_ids = fused[-1].squeeze(-1)  # [B]
488	        positions_vec = inputs["position_ids"][:, -1].to(next_pos_ids.device)
489	
490	        # Number of tokens generated this step
491	        generated_token_counts = (next_pos_ids - positions_vec).to(torch.long)
492	
493	        # Mask tail with -1 so runner can strip pads
494	        B, T = accepted_tokens_with_padding.shape
495	        generated_token_counts = generated_token_counts.clamp_(0, T)
496	
497	        masked = accepted_tokens_with_padding.clone()
498	        for b in range(B):
499	            masked[b, generated_token_counts[b] :] = -1
500	
501	        elapsed_ms = (time.perf_counter() - start_time) * 1000
502	        logger.debug(
503	            "[PERF]     _remask_fused_spec_output: %.2fms [batch=%d]", elapsed_ms, B
504	        )
505	
506	        return masked
507	
508	    def forward(self, input_ids, input_block_ids, **kwargs):
509	        forward_start = time.perf_counter()
510	        batch_size = (
511	            input_ids.shape[0]
512	            if isinstance(input_ids, torch.Tensor)
513	            else len(input_ids)
514	        )
515	
516	        with self._reordered(input_block_ids, input_ids=input_ids, **kwargs) as (
517	            sorted_ids,
518	            inputs,
519	            restore,
520	        ):
521	            # Time model execution
522	            model_start = time.perf_counter()
523	            output = self.model(
524	                inputs["input_ids"],
525	                attention_mask=None,
526	                seq_ids=sorted_ids,
527	                block_table=inputs["block_tables"],
528	                **{
529	                    k: v
530	                    for k, v in inputs.items()
531	                    if k
532	                    not in ["input_ids", "block_tables", "prefill_completion_state"]
533	                },
534	            )
535	            model_elapsed = (time.perf_counter() - model_start) * 1000
536	            logger.debug("[PERF]     model_execution: %.2fms", model_elapsed)
537	
538	            # Time output processing
539	            output_proc_start = time.perf_counter()
540	            if self.model.config.neuron_config.on_device_sampling_config:
541	                output = output.hidden_states
542	                if getattr(
543	                    self.model.config.neuron_config, "enable_fused_speculation", False
544	                ):
545	                    fused = output
546	                    output = self._remask_fused_spec_output(fused, inputs)
547	            else:
548	                if self.neuron_config.is_chunked_prefill:
549	                    assert kwargs.get("prefill_completion_state") is not None
550	                    idx_for_sampling = (
551	                        kwargs["prefill_completion_state"].nonzero().flatten()
552	                    )
553	                    output = output.logits[0, idx_for_sampling, :]
554	                else:
555	                    output = output.logits[:, -1, :]
556	            output_proc_elapsed = (time.perf_counter() - output_proc_start) * 1000
557	            logger.debug("[PERF]     output_processing: %.2fms", output_proc_elapsed)
558	
559	            # Time restore operation
560	            restore_start = time.perf_counter()
561	            result = restore(output)
562	            restore_elapsed = (time.perf_counter() - restore_start) * 1000
563	            logger.debug("[PERF]     restore: %.2fms", restore_elapsed)
564	
565	            forward_elapsed = (time.perf_counter() - forward_start) * 1000
566	            logger.debug(
567	                "[PERF]   forward() total: %.2fms [batch=%d]",
568	                forward_elapsed,
569	                batch_size,
570	            )
571	
572	            return result
573	
574	    def sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> SamplerOutput | None:
575	        if self.model.config.neuron_config.on_device_sampling_config:
576	            return SamplerOutput(
577	                # The sampled tokens are expanded to 2D tensor with shape
578	                # [num_requests, 1], where each row represents one generated
579	                # token per request.
580	                sampled_token_ids=logits.unsqueeze(-1),
581	                logprobs_tensors=None,
582	            )
583	        else:
584	            # CPU sampling is now handled by the model runner
585	            # This should not be called when on_device_sampling_config is None
586	            # as the model runner will use its own CPU sampler
587	            raise RuntimeError(
588	                "CPU sampling should be handled by the model runner, not the model. "
589	                "This indicates a bug in the sampling path routing."
590	            )
591	
592	    def load_weights(self, model_name_or_path: str, architecture: str, **kwargs):
593	        neuronx_model_cls = _get_neuron_model_cls(architecture)
594	        success, compiled_model_path, config = self._load_weights_common(
595	            model_name_or_path, neuronx_model_cls, **kwargs
596	        )
597	
598	        if not success:
599	            if not os.path.exists(model_name_or_path):
600	                model_name_or_path = self._save_pretrained_model(model_name_or_path)
601	            self._compile_and_load_model(
602	                model_name_or_path, neuronx_model_cls, config, compiled_model_path
603	            )
604	        return success, compiled_model_path
605	
606	
607	class NeuronMultiModalCausalLM(NeuronCausalLM):
608	    def load_weights(self, model_name_or_path: str, architecture: str, **kwargs):
609	        neuronx_model_cls = _get_neuron_model_cls(architecture)
610	
611	        # Neuron ImageToText model configs have nested text and vision config
612	        # each has their own neuron_config. The structure looks like:
613	        # ImageToTextInferenceConfig
614	        # ├── text_config
615	        # |   ├── text_neuron_config
616	        # |   |   └── ... ...
617	        # |   ├── text_config_arg0
618	        # |   └── ... ...
619	        # ├── vision_config
620	        # |   ├── vision_neuron_config
621	        # |   |   └── ... ...
622	        # |   ├── vision_config_arg0
623	        # |   └── ... ...
624	        # └── neuron_config (default to same as text_neuron_config)
625	        # so we override text and vision neuron_config individually
626	
627	        default_neuron_config = kwargs["neuron_config"]
628	        override_neuron_config = _validate_image_to_text_override_neuron_config(
629	            kwargs["override_neuron_config"]
630	        )
631	
632	        vision_neuron_config = copy.deepcopy(default_neuron_config)
633	        vision_neuron_config.update(
634	            override_neuron_config.get("vision_neuron_config", {})
635	        )
636	        vision_neuron_config = neuronx_model_cls.get_neuron_config_cls()(
637	            **vision_neuron_config
638	        )
639	
640	        text_neuron_config = copy.deepcopy(default_neuron_config)
641	        text_neuron_config.update(override_neuron_config.get("text_neuron_config", {}))
642	        text_neuron_config = neuronx_model_cls.get_neuron_config_cls()(
643	            **text_neuron_config
644	        )
645	
646	        config = neuronx_model_cls.get_config_cls()(
647	            text_neuron_config=text_neuron_config,
648	            vision_neuron_config=vision_neuron_config,
649	            load_config=load_pretrained_config(model_name_or_path),
650	        )
651	
652	        success, compiled_model_path, _ = self._load_weights_common(
653	            model_name_or_path, neuronx_model_cls, config=config, **kwargs
654	        )
655	
656	        if not success:
657	            if not os.path.exists(model_name_or_path):
658	                model_name_or_path = self._save_pretrained_model(model_name_or_path)
659	
660	            self._compile_and_load_model(
661	                model_name_or_path, neuronx_model_cls, config, compiled_model_path
662	            )
663	        return success, compiled_model_path
664	
665	    def execute_model(self, model_input, **kwargs):
666	        """Helper to run model with multimodal inputs."""
667	
668	        pixel_values = None
669	        if (
670	            model_input.multi_modal_kwargs is not None
671	            and model_input.multi_modal_kwargs.get("pixel_values") is not None
672	        ):
673	            pixel_values = model_input.multi_modal_kwargs["pixel_values"]
674	
675	        hidden_states = self.forward(
676	            input_ids=model_input.input_tokens,
677	            positions=model_input.position_ids,
678	            input_block_ids=model_input.input_block_ids,
679	            sampling_params=model_input.sampling_params,
680	            pixel_values=pixel_values,
681	            **kwargs,
682	        )
683	        return hidden_states

685	    def forward(
686	        self,
687	        input_ids: torch.Tensor,
688	        positions: torch.Tensor,
689	        input_block_ids: torch.Tensor,
690	        sampling_params: torch.Tensor,
691	        pixel_values: torch.Tensor | None = None,
692	        vision_mask: torch.Tensor | None = None,
693	        **kwargs,
694	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
695	        """Forward pass with multimodal support for multi-modal model."""
696	        with self._reordered(
697	            input_block_ids,
698	            input_ids=input_ids,
699	            positions=positions,
700	            sampling_params=sampling_params,
701	            pixel_values=pixel_values,
702	            vision_mask=vision_mask,
703	            **kwargs,
704	        ) as (sorted_ids, inputs, restore):
705	            output = self.model(
706	                inputs["input_ids"].to(torch.int32),
707	                attention_mask=None,
708	                position_ids=inputs["positions"].to(torch.int32),
709	                seq_ids=sorted_ids.flatten().to(torch.int32),
710	                pixel_values=inputs.get("pixel_values"),
711	                vision_mask=inputs.get("vision_mask"),
712	                sampling_params=inputs["sampling_params"],
713	                **kwargs,
714	            )
715	
716	            if self.model.config.neuron_config.on_device_sampling_config:
717	                output = output.hidden_states
718	            else:
719	                output = output.logits[:, -1, :]
720	
721	            return restore(output)

724	class NeuronPixtralForCausalLM(NeuronMultiModalCausalLM):
725	    def execute_model(self, model_input):
726	        """Helper to run model with defaults for missing multimodal inputs."""
727	        vision_mask = (
728	            model_input.input_tokens == self.model.config.image_token_index
729	        ).unsqueeze(-1)
730	
731	        if (
732	            model_input.multi_modal_kwargs is not None
733	            and model_input.multi_modal_kwargs.get("pixel_values") is not None
734	        ):
735	            image_sizes = model_input.multi_modal_kwargs.get("image_sizes")
736	        else:
737	            image_sizes = torch.tensor([[512, 512]], dtype=torch.int32)

741	        )

743	    def forward(
744	        self,
745	        input_ids: torch.Tensor,
746	        positions: torch.Tensor,
747	        input_block_ids: torch.Tensor,
748	        sampling_params: torch.Tensor,
749	        pixel_values: Union[torch.Tensor, list] | None = None,
750	        image_sizes: torch.Tensor | None = None,
751	        vision_mask: torch.Tensor | None = None,
752	        **kwargs,
753	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
754	        """Forward pass with multimodal support."""
755	
756	        # Cast vision tensors to the configured dtype
757	        if pixel_values is not None:
758	            dtype = self.model.config.vision_config.neuron_config.torch_dtype
759	            if isinstance(pixel_values, torch.Tensor):

761	            elif isinstance(pixel_values, list):

763	
764	        with self._reordered(
765	            input_block_ids,
766	            input_ids=input_ids,
767	            positions=positions,
768	            sampling_params=sampling_params,
769	            pixel_values=pixel_values,
770	            vision_mask=vision_mask,
771	            image_sizes=image_sizes,
772	        ) as (sorted_ids, inputs, restore):
773	            output = self.model(
774	                inputs["input_ids"].to(torch.int32),
775	                attention_mask=None,
776	                position_ids=inputs["positions"].to(torch.int32),
777	                seq_ids=sorted_ids.flatten().to(torch.int32),
778	                pixel_values=inputs.get("pixel_values"),
779	                vision_mask=inputs.get("vision_mask"),
780	                sampling_params=inputs["sampling_params"],
781	                image_sizes=inputs.get("image_sizes"),
782	            )
783	
784	            if self.model.config.neuron_config.on_device_sampling_config:
785	                output = output.hidden_states
786	            else:
787	                output = output.logits[:, -1, :]
788	
789	            return restore(output)

863	class NeuronLlama4ForCausalLM(NeuronMultiModalCausalLM):
864	    def __init__(self, config):
865	        super().__init__(config)
866	        self.vision_token_id = None
867	
868	    def load_weights(self, model_name_or_path: str, architecture: str, **kwargs):
869	        success, compiled_model_path = super().load_weights(
870	            model_name_or_path, architecture, **kwargs
871	        )
872	
873	        # Load tokenizer to get vision token ID
874	        from transformers import AutoTokenizer
875	
876	        tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_name_or_path)
877	        self.vision_token_id = tokenizer(
878	            "<|image|>", add_special_tokens=False
879	        ).input_ids[0]
880	        return success, compiled_model_path
881	
882	    def forward(
883	        self,
884	        input_ids: torch.Tensor,
885	        positions: torch.Tensor,
886	        input_block_ids: torch.Tensor,
887	        sampling_params: torch.Tensor,
888	        pixel_values: torch.Tensor | None = None,
889	        vision_mask: torch.Tensor | None = None,
890	        **kwargs,
891	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
892	        """Forward pass with multimodal support for Llama4."""
893	
894	        if pixel_values is not None:
895	            logger.debug("pixel_values.shape = %s", pixel_values.shape)
896	            if len(pixel_values.shape) == 5:
897	                bsz, n_chunks, n_channels, h, w = (
898	                    pixel_values.shape
899	                )  # (1, 5, 3, 336, 336)
900	                pixel_values = pixel_values.reshape(
901	                    bsz * n_chunks, n_channels, h, w
902	                )  # (5, 3, 336, 336)
903	                pixel_values = pixel_values.to(torch.bfloat16)
904	        if pixel_values is not None and vision_mask is None:
905	            vision_mask = (input_ids == self.model.config.image_token_index).unsqueeze(
906	                -1
907	            )
908	
909	        if vision_mask is not None:
910	            vision_mask = vision_mask.to(torch.bool)
911	
912	        # Ensure sampling params match input batch size
913	        if input_ids.shape[0] != sampling_params.shape[0]:
914	            sampling_params = sampling_params[: input_ids.shape[0]]
915	
916	        return super().forward(
917	            input_ids,
918	            positions,
919	            input_block_ids,
920	            sampling_params,
921	            pixel_values,
922	            vision_mask,
923	            **kwargs,
924	        )

927	def _get_model_configs(config: PretrainedConfig) -> str:
928	    logger.debug("PretrainedConfig: %s", config)
929	
930	    archs = getattr(config, "architectures", [])
931	    if not archs:
932	        raise ValueError("No architectures specified in the pretrained config.")
933	    architecture = archs[0]
934	    if architecture in NEURON_MULTI_MODAL_MODELS:
935	        config = getattr(config, "text_config", None)
936	    num_key_value_heads = getattr(config, "num_key_value_heads", None)
937	    head_dim = getattr(config, "head_dim", None)
938	    if not head_dim:
939	        num_attention_heads = getattr(config, "num_attention_heads", None)
940	        hidden_size = getattr(config, "hidden_size", None)
941	        if num_attention_heads and hidden_size:
942	            head_dim = hidden_size // num_attention_heads
943	    if not num_key_value_heads:
944	        raise ValueError(
945	            "Missing required num_key_value_heads in the pretrained config."
946	        )
947	    if not head_dim:
948	        raise ValueError("Missing required head dimension in the pretrained config.")
949	    return architecture, int(num_key_value_heads), int(head_dim)

952	def _camel_to_kebab(name: str) -> str:
953	    s1 = re.sub("(.)([A-Z][a-z]+)", r"\1-\2", name)
954	    return re.sub("([a-z0-9])([A-Z])", r"\1-\2", s1).lower()
955
